gradator methodology applied. The numbers in the table are real, computed through the live
book on the probe window.

| bdw | p_dir Ōēź 0.80 AND p_opp Ōēż 0.05 | clean direction |

| LONG88 | side=long, p_dir Ōēź 0.88 | higher bar (longs run) |

## Verdict: max-turnover does NOT work (6th confirmation of the volume wall)

- **Almost everything is negative $/day.** The biggest turnover (RAW80, SHORT80; 140ŌĆō324
  trades) is the biggest bleed (ŌłÆ5ŌĆ”ŌłÆ7 $/day). Only tiny high-bar slices are in the green
  (LONG88 5ŌĆō21 trades; d8 SPREAD70 35 trades) ŌĆö and those barely, on small n.
- **Win-rates of 39ŌĆō54%** = nearly a coin flip, and the cost (RT ~0.13%) finishes it off.
- This is exactly **the wall from CLAUDE.md ┬¦2**: loosening the bar for volume ALWAYS kills
  the win-rate ŌåÆ into the losing zone. Frequency multiplies exposure, it does not dilute
  losses.
- **And this is on the probe, which is itself optimistic** ŌĆö on the exam/live it would be
  worse.
- **bdw is practically empty**: the binance models almost never produce p_opp Ōēż 0.05 (unlike
  the old OKX ones). This filter doesn't apply here.

## What it means / where next
"Net through quantity" via a **low bar is dead** (once again). The real route to volume
(CLAUDE.md ┬¦3): not a lower threshold, but **more SPACE at the same high bar** ŌĆö more symbols
├Ś several legs per (symbol, scan) ├Ś an ensemble. That is, RAW85/SPREAD on 60+ symbols +
multi-leg, not RAW80 on a wider gate.

## Caveat
Everything is on ONE window (probe). Even the optimistic probe couldn't pass max-turnover ŌĆö
but for final conclusions each build is worth an AŌåÆB look in the browser. The builds are
left in the folder for manual research; as a money strategy, max-turnover did not pass the
probe.
